content:
finding: expMs<nowMs triggers 401 without skew allowance
exploit: token at boundary loops 401/refresh each tick
mitigation: Δ cmp → expMs < (nowMs - 30_000)
site: auth/ expiry middleware, single comparison
invariants:
- skew budget = 30s, encoded once
- no widen beyond 30s
- `next(err)` path unchanged
- `401` on fail, no new trust on `x-user-id`
verification:
- unit: expMs == nowMs - 30_000 → pass
- unit: expMs == nowMs - 30_001 → `401`
- unit: expMs == nowMs → pass (within skew)
- unit: expMs == nowMs + 1 → pass
- integration: boundary token no refresh loop
- missing token → `401`
- malformed payload → `401`
- unit-mismatch (s vs ms) → `401`
risks:
- ! skew constant duplicated elsewhere
- ! refresh path uses different cmp
- ? ms normalization upstream assumed
A: single-site cmp patch + 30s skew constant + boundary tests

A: modular_monolith
why: team(6) ∧ ddl(4m) ∧ ops(pt) ⇒ low_ops_budget
store: PostgreSQL
split: defer → post_release
seams: clean_module_bounds ∧ stable_APIs ∧ no_shared_mutable_state
R: ! premature_distribution

finding: public boundary now trusts `x-user-id`, overrides session
severity: high
class: session confusion, horizontal privilege escalation

exploit:
attacker sends `x-user-id: <victim>` header
middleware picks header over `session.userId`
downstream scopes data by attacker-chosen id
cross-user read/write

invariant broken: `session.userId` authoritative at public edge
invariant broken: no trust on client headers at public boundary

mitigation:
revert to `const userId = session.userId;`
keep `if(!userId) return res.status(401).end();`
if internal-proxy identity needed, gate on signed gateway assertion in `gateway/`, strip client `x-user-id` at edge, never fall back to header on public route

verify:
test: spoof `x-user-id` with valid session → resolved id == `session.userId`
test: spoof `x-user-id` with no session → `401`
test: victim session + attacker `x-user-id` → no cross-user access
test: missing both → `401`
test: header stripped at `gateway/` before `auth/`

risks:
regression on any internal route relying on header fallback
confirm no mobile/first-party client depends on `x-user-id` override
audit other middleware for same pattern

G: refactor(loadUser) → async/await
C: preserve(order) ∧ preserve(next(err)) ∧ minimal_change

P:
1. validate(missing_id) → next(err)
2. await db.findUser(id)
3. branch(!user) → next(err) [not_found]
4. await audit.log(user)
5. await cache.set(user)
6. attach(req.user) → next()

Invariants:
- single error path = next(err)
- no signature change on loadUser(req,res,next)
- db_err → next(err) via try/catch
- audit.log + cache.set remain post-load, pre-response
- no parallel Promise.all (order preserved)

Sketch:
```
async function loadUser(req, res, next) {
  try {
    const id = req.params.id
    if (!id) return next(err_missing_id)
    const user = await db.findUser(id)
    if (!user) return next(err_not_found)
    await audit.log(user)
    await cache.set(user)
    req.user = user
    next()
  } catch (err) {
    next(err)
  }
}
```

Risks:
- ! db.findUser may use callback API → wrap via util.promisify, not direct await
- ! `this` binding on db.findUser → bind or arrow-wrap
- ! audit.log / cache.set if callback-style → same wrap needed
- ! double next() if catch fires after partial success → guard with return on each next()

V:
- test: missing id → next(err_missing_id), no db call
- test: db.findUser rejects → next(err), no audit, no cache

root_cause:
- setInterval callback closes over batch rows array
- each tick appends, never clears reference
- closure retains prior batches even when queue empty
- heapUsed grows monotonic across ticks

min_fix:
- move batch rows declaration inside tick handler
- or null reference after flush: `rows = null`
- preserve setInterval signature
- preserve next(err) path

reg_test:
- run worker with empty queue N ticks
- assert heapUsed delta < threshold after GC
- assert no retained batch refs via heap snapshot diff
- boundary: exact 0-row tick, 1-row tick, flush-then-idle

risks:
- ! closure capture elsewhere in same module
- ? timer handle itself retained by module scope
- this-binding if rows method moved

verify:
- heap snapshot before/after idle window
- node --expose-gc + global.gc() between ticks

finding: spoofable rate-limit key via `X-Forwarded-For`
exploit: attacker sets arbitrary `X-Forwarded-For` → unique keys per request → bypass limit; or pin victim IP → exhaust victim quota
mitigation:
- trust only gateway-normalized client IP
- parse `X-Forwarded-For` leftmost-after-trusted-hops, not raw header
- fallback `req.ip` only when behind trusted proxy (`app.set('trust proxy', <hops>)`)
- never use raw client header at public boundary
patch sketch: `const ip = resolveClientIp(req); const key = \`rl:${ip}\`;`
verify:
- test: spoofed `X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4` → key derives from real edge IP, not header
- test: chained `X-Forwarded-For: a,b,c` → picks correct hop per trust config
- test: missing header → `req.ip` used only under trusted proxy
- test: same attacker, rotated header values → single key, limit enforced
- test: victim IP in header by attacker → victim quota unaffected
risks:
- ! misconfigured `trust proxy` re-opens spoof
- ! Redis key cardinality blowup if spoof not blocked
- ? shared-NAT false positives on legit users

dedup_strategy:
- table `webhook_events(key TEXT PRIMARY KEY, provider TEXT, payload_hash BYTEA, status TEXT, response_body JSONB, received_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ)`
- key = `Idempotency-Key` header; fallback `provider:event_id` if absent
- `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (key) DO NOTHING RETURNING ...`
- insert wins → process → update `status=done`, store `response_body`
- insert loses → load row → branch on `status`
- `status=done` → replay stored `response_body`, same status code
- `status=pending` → return `409` (in-flight)
- `payload_hash` mismatch on same key → `409` conflict, log
- processing inside single transaction; side effects via outbox row in same tx

error_model:
- `200` replay on completed duplicate
- `409` on in-flight duplicate or payload mismatch
- `5xx` on transient downstream → leave `status=pending`, provider retries
- `4xx` on malformed → `status=failed_permanent`, no retry path
- single `next(err)` mapping site preserved

short_why:
- provider retries on `5xx` → must dedupe by stable key
- `ON CONFLICT` = atomic first-writer-wins
- stored response = deterministic replay
- `payload_hash` guards key reuse abuse
- outbox in same tx = no lost side effects
- `pending` vs `done` split = observable replay state

finding: N+1 query db.findUser per row
cause: per-row lookup inside invoice loop
fix: batch-load users before loop
  - collect unique customerId set
  - single query: db.findUsers(ids)
  - build Map<id,user>
  - resolve row.customerId via Map
invariant: response shape unchanged
invariant: order preserved
invariant: null customer → same null handling as before

min_fix:
  - pre-loop: ids = unique(rows.map(r => r.customerId))
  - pre-loop: userMap = new Map((await db.findUsers(ids)).map(u => [u.id, u]))
  - in-loop: user = userMap.get(row.customerId) ?? null

reg_test:
  - seed N=50 invoices, M=10 distinct customers
  - assert db query count ≤ 2 (invoices + users batch)
  - assert response body deep-equals pre-fix snapshot
  - assert null-customer row still renders null user

risks:
  - db.findUsers missing → add thin helper, no schema change
  - large id set → chunk at 1000
  - duplicate ids already deduped via Set

verify:
  - query counter assertion in test
  - p95 latency check /invoices < pre-regression baseline
